question: What is the primary goal of refactoring {{target}}?
plain_language_preface: We're refactoring {{target}}. Clarifying the primary goal focuses the refactor scope and prevents scope creep that inflates stage cost.

options.label: Extract — pull shared logic into a reusable unit
options.tradeoff: High reuse value; new abstraction must be named + documented.

options.label: Inline — collapse thin wrappers / remove indirection
options.tradeoff: Reduces call-graph depth; harder to test in isolation after.

options.label: Rename + reorganise — improve discoverability with no logic change
options.tradeoff: Zero behaviour change; high diff noise for reviewers.

options.label: Split — divide oversized unit into focused sub-units
options.tradeoff: Better SRP; callers must be updated to new split surface.

options.label: Defer — log tech-debt note, skip refactor this stage
options.tradeoff: Zero stage cost; debt row added to backlog.

recommended_rationale: Extraction has the highest reuse ROI and is testable in isolation; inline (B) only when the abstraction adds zero value.
